The genus Rubus includes a group of flowering plants commonly known as brambles or thorny shrubs, which are part of the rose family (Rosaceae). The health benefits of Rubus species, such as Rubus idaeus (raspberry) and Rubus fruticosus (blackberry), include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against various diseases, including heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Rubus species include flavonoids, anthocyanins, and ellagic acid, among others, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rubus species include teas, tinctures, and jams, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
